title: Todos — BroMenu spec, late 2024
html:
Todos — BroMenu spec, late 2024

Source: 2024-12-21 Apple Note, last modified 2024-12-31 — captures the seed moment when Brotein is still being called BroMenu and Yahir is writing his first real product spec for it. Bridges the late-2024 gap between departing China and the mid-2025 operational grind.

TL;DR

Three layers stacked into one note:

  1. Tiny todo header — "add better loading animations / get the phone updated / get the first physical phone testing going / print out menu for testing." This is the work-in-progress on the prototype.
  2. A single word — "Iguazú" — sitting alone, no context. Travel daydream? Future destination? See iguazu.
  3. Full product spec for BroMenu — the most explicit, structured product doc in the corpus to date. Reads like a PRD Yahir wrote to brief himself before coding.

The BroMenu spec (key extract)

"A fitness-focused iOS mobile app for identifying the most anabolic menu items from restaurant menus, helping users achieve muscle building, bulking, or cutting goals."

Stack:

  • Frontend: SwiftUI in WindSurf IDE, tested via Xcode on a physical iPhone (explicitly to avoid the Apple Developer fee).
  • AI: OpenAI Camera API for OCR, GPT API for anabolic scoring of items.
  • Backend: Supabase for storage + "Sign in with Apple" auth.
  • UI inspiration: AliPay / WeChat QR scan flows — direct callback to China year.

He notes: "I am starting from absolutely nothing, from scratch. I need to figure out my workflow and have to test the functionality that I am building." — explicit beginner's-mind framing.

Test-plan substructure

Yahir wrote out a full QA matrix: camera permissions, throttling ("Slow down, bro!"), error scenarios (covered lens, blur, poor lighting), menu varieties (restaurant, cafe, handwritten, digital boards), Supabase integration, API quota, memory leaks via Instruments, battery drain, cellular data usage, interruption handling (calls, backgrounding, low battery, airplane mode). For a solo founder pre-launch, this is unusually thorough — closer to what a QA lead would write.

Quotes

"Mimic the sleek, real-time QR scanning experience of AliPay or WeChat, including animations and immediate feedback."

"Within Xcode I will be connecting a physical device to test the camera functionality and also because I want to avoid paying for the apple developer fee."

"Reduce API call costs by caching responses wherever possible."

The cost-consciousness (no runway discipline) shows up even in technical decisions.

Open questions

  • Did the BroMenu name become Brotein, or were they sibling concepts that converged? Brotein still references "menu parallel scanning" in 2025 todos — same lineage.
  • Did "Iguazú" ever get followed up on, or was it pure daydream?
  • Is the late-2024 BroMenu prototype the same codebase that became 2025 Brotein, or a throwaway?

Privacy note

The raw file ends with three credentials: an OPENAI_API_KEY (looks intact), a SUPABASE_URL, and a SUPABASE_KEY which is already [REDACTED-SUPABASE-KEY]. The OpenAI key in the raw file looks like it may NOT have been redacted — flag for Yahir to rotate / scrub. Per CLAUDE.md, never edit raw/, but he should be aware. Also worth scanning git history for any pre-redaction commits.
